reviews.review_text:
Rats, rats, we are the rats. 
Celebrating yet another birthday bash.
Michael: It's your birthday today.
Cake and ice cream is on it's way.
And Michael: You've been such a good boy this year.
Open up your gifts while we all cheer.

After almost 900 hours of this game, there is only thing I have to say about this game : BY TAAL, FIGHT, KILL, DEATH. 

But Im gonna tell you more anyways.

The blood, gore and death, the killing, maiming and the sheer slaughter. The animalistic violence and impossible odds this game gives to you in the midst of a deep and dire setting is a gift that only the warhammer franchise can give to you. This game is perfect, despite the flaws and a few funky gamemodes. It was my gateway drug into Warhammer Fantasy as a whole, but instead of moving to new drugs, I simply cant stay away from this specific one: The Slaughter of the Pactsworn and all that worships Chaos.

It merely promises you one thing and that is: Chaotic Slaughter from the view of 5 very different people. (Really great gameplay, characters and lore too)

So by Sigmar, Taal, Grimnir ,Isha, and Myrmidia do your duty to push back The Tide.

Christ bless you.
This game was fun and visually interesting but it gave me motion sickness, will not be playing anymore.

Let me start by saying this is my first Warhammer game. I picked it up on recommendation from a friend.

Warhammer: Vermintide 2 has quickly become one of my favorite co‑op horde games. The combat feels weighty and satisfying, the missions are varied, and the enemy density creates a level of intensity that is hard to find elsewhere. It is a game that rewards teamwork and communication, but it also leaves plenty of room for individual playstyle.

I have spent most of my time playing Kerillian in her Handmaiden career, and it has been a great experience. Her mobility and fluid combat style make her incredibly fun to play. Dashing through enemies, supporting the team, and weaving in and out of danger feels smooth and rewarding.

The co‑op aspect is where the game truly shines. Every run feels different, and even the chaotic moments are enjoyable. The world design, sound effects, and overall atmosphere are impressive and help bring the Warhammer setting to life. Whether you are fighting through dark tunnels or open battlefields, the game always feels immersive.

If you enjoy co‑op games with strong combat and a lot of replay value, Vermintide 2 is an easy recommendation.
Extremely fun, some level grinding required but not bad at all. Great quickplay system encourages just dropping into matches with strangers, and a mostly positive community. Strongly recommend.

Rat game 2

It is fun, although devs started making DLCs using "introduce new class and then nerf it into something barely playable" strategy which isn't very nice

It's very good if you find people to do missions with but that can be complicated now since the game is almost 10 years old

bots are really bad and fatshark doesnt allow mods that improve their AI. game is too old to consistently fill lobbies, so the least fatshark could do is sanction the popular mod to fix their bad bot AI.

I enjoyed it before all the "God Like Power" Dlc's

Even the fire mage class becomes to Op to undermine later

All the more, still a solid entry if you're curious as to what people will do once you disable their "fight or flight respone"

Lol this game is also living proof as to why i trust none

-Eshin

Almost 10 years later and this marvel still hits hard... and you don’t even need a beefy PC to run this phenomenon.

The detail... Bon appétit!

Maps... Characters... Cosomization... ...Squisito!

And again, the graphics and gameplay are so uniquely integrated. I played it on a modern laptop with no dedicated graphics card, and the gameplay was smooth with beautiful graphics.

I like Vermintide 2 for almost every reason I like Darktide, which I suppose isn't very surprising when you look at it, the two are very similar. This game is definitely the better choice if you like something slightly slower paced and/or if your PC doesn't quite reach Darktide's recommended specs.

LINUX COMPATIBILITY: Game runs perfectly via Proton Experimental if the Game Version is set to temporary_launcher_workaround.
I LOVE people in this game so much. So friendly(mostly). But still It is worth playing .
One of the most fun co-op games I have played. Melee combat is solid and the weapons are extremely satisfying. I enjoy slaying rats with my bros.
It's fun to play with, especially with friends, but when you're alone it's a bit boring, but over all it's good.
One of the best game you can find especially when its on sale. In my opinion its a timeless classic. At the moment of me posting this review its %95 discount so I say don't miss it.

if you like the gameplay of left 4 dead but melee focused and more character/class focused with a deep progression this game is the bomb. i like it more than darktide simply because, its more melee focused, the maps/campaigns have variance and im not just stuck in a industrial factory (darktide) and each character/class plays rather different. also its had the years and years of balance changes. i dunno i just keep coming back to it.

Fun and satisfying gameplay loop coming in, and a challenging system to master! if you want to just bathe in the blood of hundreds of ratmen larping as a drunken dwarf you can, and if you'd like to test your mettle against near endless elites and specials- you can do that too. There is so much depth from the character interactions, map secrets, and a  skill ceiling where I still find myself finding a new strategy for a career I haven't touched much. 
There still is a lot of fatshark jank (if ykyk)

Satisfying and intense combat, simple to pick up and very hard to master, with enough build variety to feel different but not overwhelming. Truly, a game to play with the boys or randos, hard to go wrong

First person slasher.  Great game for running around with a sword and mowing down hordes.

The apex of Fatshark's design, Vermintide 2 is more polished than its previous iteration *and* its spacefaring successor, with interesting roguelike options in chaos wastes, more strategic but still fast-paced combat, interesting and varied missions and story, varied character builds and classes exploring the wealth of weirdness in the warhammer universe, and wonderfully diverse and cutting banter between characters. 

It doesn't (usually) overwhelm or spam specials, but provides them at just the right times and places to be challenging. Its hordes aren't infinite unless you stay in one place for too long. The difficulty and precision required to survive are challenging but don't usually feel unfair. Its cosmetics are varied and interesting, and quite a few granted for free just by playing the game.
Great melee-focused Left 4 Dead-eqsue game still with some ongoing support. Loads of content at this point, although Chaos Wastes is long due for some more maps.
Rats. Its like Left 4 Dead, funny characters, fun combat and gameplay. Played this a lot on Xbox.

One of the best games I have ever played. All the classes are unique, and the DLC characters aren’t too pricey. The game still has a good number of players, making it easy to find 4-man groups. I totally recommend it whether you are playing solo or with friends.
Runs well, lots of content, usually great randoms to play with.
Great Game. The game is a horde shooter at its core. Best game of its type. Plenty of game modes and tons of re-playability. Smooth gameplay with in depth melee combat.
Great game to get on sale. The difficulty scaling is crazy
Amazing game to play solo and even better to play with friends

I didn't like Vermintide.
But this isn't that.
This is the same game as Darktide, but a little less of the constant pressure, and a little more laid-back, and a little less random.
It's a low-ish fantasy, mostly hack and slash, co-op horde hero shooter.

The heroes are mostly quite distinct from one-another, and each hero has several different class options. A lot of the classes fall back on frenzied melee combat with the same weapons, but each hero has at least one class that is genuinely unique and interesting. Well, almost all...
There's some decent talents and builds available that change the way a given class plays pretty noticeably, but overall the talents are a little lackluster. Most of them are pretty pointless filler on their way to something actually interesting.
Same for the weapons. A lot of the weapons feel pretty similar, and the buffs and such on them rarely make much difference. But sometimes they make a HUGE difference for certain classes and builds, so...

There's a lot to do, lots of cosmetics, daily/weekly tasks to earn them, different challenge modes, Fatshark even still releases new content occasionally for it.

My only complaints, which honestly were almost enough to make me not recommend it, are that each hero has a class that you have to buy and can't earn, and the specialist enemies can instantly incapacitate you until your team saves you or you die.
Darktide makes it easier to see/hear those guys coming and gives them a little more windup so you can counter/dodge them in a way that feels much less unfair.

But overall it's a fun game, even solo, but moreso with friends.
If you like games like left 4 dead or payday 2, then you're going to love this one. Co-op game with rpg mechanics adding a ton of replayability.
Slay the heretic, the witch, and the unclean! In Sigmar's name!

Honestly. The game itself is good. But the easy anti cheat makes this game a pain in the butt to play on the steam deck. But to be fair, it says unsupported on the steam bag but it really doesn't need to be. Plus they'll sell you the game on the steam deck anyway so hey, you got to take the criticism too. 

The other thing I hate about this game is the fact that you cannot play offline. On console you can play offline but on PC you can't. For some reason console has the superior version if you're just purely playing without any mods. And honestly, we know why there's no offline mode, it's so fat sharp can harvest your data and sell it to their affiliates.

Lastly, vermintide is just kind of not that great. It's essentially the worst parts of a story mode where in order to continue, they throw an endless horde of PVE enemies at you with not enough gameplay variety to keep things interesting. 

 I don't recommend the game on pc, if you're going to pick it up, I'd say grab them console.

Nice setting and it really could be a great game, but there are some severe issues...

Combat: Melee feels absolutely empty, weightless, hollow...it´s just smooth overspeed sliding from here to there while clicking like crazy. Nothing feels physical or massive on anything at any scale; This game has the same "melee-feeling" as the original Doom ´93. Don´t let fool yourself by the screenshots: It´s just an empty pale skin of what close quarter combat really is. (Fatekeeper does it parsecs better...I know, 8 years & so, but solide combat wasn´t wizzardy even back then and if you can´t do it right for sale, then don´t do it all...go back and practice, it´s not some private fun project, ffs.)

Too much ingame-merch: Nuff said.

Lack of physics...See "Combat" and moving in the world in general.

Frustration...'Cause you expected more. Something serious, Something finished.

If you´re a fan of the franchise and don´t give a damn about anything above: Go, get it!

For everyone else: Avoid!
played a lot on xbox but cannot play on steamos. would love anticheat to be fixed to work with linux.

I got a key from 3rd website for like 4 dollars, and it was a best game buy looking from like a one 1 year view. Last purchase that was that good is probably Path of Exile, but for 4 dollars? This game is 3 times better than a PoE2
It's still getting new content even now. Phenomenal level of dev support.
The banter is grade A material. Never fails to get a chuckle whilst wading through a lake of rats and filth.
Darktide might have it narrowly beat in 2026 if you only care about the second-to-second combat, in virtually every other regard this is still by far the superior Warhammer horde clicker.
Simple to play, hard to master, good clean fun.
